VOLLAND SPX GREEK HEDGING
GREEK HEDGING (SPX) ESTIMATES THE DIRECTION AND SIZE OF DAILY DEALER REBALANCING FLOWS IMPLIED BY THE OPTIONS MARKET.

DELTA HEDGING (~$15.52B): SUGGESTS DEALERS MAY NEED TO BUY UNDERLYING (OR FUTURES) TO STAY HEDGED AGAINST PRICE MOVES.
VEGA HEDGING (~-$7.06B): EXPOSURE TO CHANGES IN IMPLIED VOLATILITY; TYPICALLY MANAGED THROUGH REPRICING OPTIONS, WITH THE NEGATIVE FIGURE SUGGESTING VOLATILITY SENSITIVITY IS DECLINING.
THETA HEDGING (~$0.52M): THE IMPACT OF ONE DAY PASSING ON THE DEALER BOOK; LIKE VEGA, THIS IS LARGELY HANDLED THROUGH OPTION REPRICING, WITH MINIMAL ADDITIONAL HEDGING PRESSURE FROM TIME DECAY.
GREEK HEDGING: NET NOTIONAL DEALER HEDGING THAT NEEDS TO BE APPLIED BY THE END OF THE DAY
